content: Neuroinflammation refers to inflammation within the central nervous system, involving activation of microglia (brain immune cells) and astrocytes, with release of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Chronic neuroinflammation is increasingly implicated in depression, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and neurodegenerative diseases. Elevated inflammatory markers (CRP, IL-6, TNF-alpha) are found in subsets of depressed patients. Inflammation can reduce serotonin availability, impair neuroplasticity, and cause 'sickness behavior' resembling depression. Anti-inflammatory agents show modest antidepressant effects in trials. Obesity, stress, and infection can trigger neuroinflammation. This paradigm supports personalized approaches targeting inflammation in treatment-resistant cases.
